source.title: An Act Concerning Personal Data Privacy and Online Monitoring
source.verification_method: Extracted from Connecticut General Statutes Chapter 746aa
source.disclaimer: Consult Connecticut Attorney General for enforcement guidance.

sections.title: Definitions
sections.text: Consumer means natural person residing in Connecticut acting in individual or household context. Controller means person determining purposes and means of processing personal data. Personal data means information linked or linkable to identified or identifiable natural person. Sensitive data means personal data revealing racial or ethnic origin, religious beliefs, mental or physical health condition, sex life, sexual orientation, citizenship status, genetic or biometric data, or precise geolocation.

sections.title: Applicability
sections.text: Applies to persons conducting business in Connecticut or targeting products/services to Connecticut residents, that during calendar year: (1) Control or process personal data of at least 100,000 consumers; or (2) Control or process personal data of at least 25,000 consumers and derive over 25% of gross revenue from sale of personal data.

sections.title: Consumer rights
sections.text: Consumers have rights to: (1) Confirm whether controller is processing personal data and access such data; (2) Correct inaccuracies; (3) Delete personal data; (4) Obtain copy in portable format; (5) Opt out of processing for targeted advertising, sale, or profiling with legal effects.

sections.title: Controller responsibilities
sections.text: Controllers must: (1) Limit collection to adequate, relevant, and reasonably necessary; (2) Not process for incompatible purposes without consent; (3) Establish reasonable security practices; (4) Not discriminate against consumers; (5) Provide privacy notice; (6) Conduct data protection assessments for high-risk processing.

sections.title: Sensitive data consent
sections.text: Controller shall not process sensitive data without consumer consent. For consumers ages 13-16, must obtain consent. Processing must not violate anti-discrimination laws.

sections.title: Enforcement
sections.text: Exclusive enforcement by Attorney General. No private right of action. Attorney General must provide 60 days notice and opportunity to cure. Civil penalties as provided by Connecticut Unfair Trade Practices Act.
